info.title: Aleo AMM API
info.description:
REST API for the Aleo concentrated-liquidity AMM. Serves indexed pool / position / swap data, routing, swap quotes, swap-settlement status, and on-chain input schemas for building transactions against the deployment-configured shield_swap program and `token_registry.aleo`.

## Numeric wire format

Exact financial values are JSON strings, not JSON numbers. Unsigned decimal inputs use ASCII digits and an optional `.` fraction, for example `"1234.50"`; base-unit integers use ASCII digits only. Grouping separators, localized decimal commas, signs, exponent notation, whitespace, Unicode digits, and redundant leading zeros are not accepted for those inputs. Signed response metrics may include a leading `-`. Clients should localize only for display and convert user input back to this canonical form before sending it.

## Authentication

Except for `/health`, `/metrics`, `/auth/*`, `/compliance*`, `GET /tokens*`, and the pool metadata reads (`GET /pools`, `GET /pools/stats`, `GET /pools/{key}`), every endpoint requires `Authorization: Bearer <credential>` from an invited wallet. The credential is either a session JWT from `/auth/verify` (24h) or a long-lived API token (`ss_…`) minted at `POST /api-tokens`. API tokens cover the data and trading surface; token management, code redemption, and admin endpoints accept JWTs only.

## Real-time WebSocket feed

Live updates are pushed over a WebSocket served by the **indexer** service at `GET /ws` (a WebSocket upgrade — a separate service from this REST API, not one of the paths below). Clients manage subscriptions with JSON frames:

```json
{"action": "authenticate", "token": "<wallet-jwt>"}
{"action": "subscribe", "room": "trades:<pool_key>"}
{"action": "unsubscribe", "room": "trades:<pool_key>"}
```

The authentication frame must be first. Balance rooms are restricted to the JWT subject.

Rooms:
- `pool_launches` — newly created pools
- `pool_stats:<pool_key>` — price & liquidity updates
- `ohlcv:<pool_key>` — candle updates
- `trades:<pool_key>` — swaps / mints / burns
- `balances:<address>` — balance-change hints for an address

Each pushed message is `{"type": <event>, "data": { … }}`, where `type` is one of `PoolLaunch`, `PoolStats`, `Ohlcv`, `Trade`, `BalanceChange`. Financial fields use the same exact string format.

422.description: Request body does not match schema
Request body does not match schema

post.description:
Mints 1000 (×10^decimals) of every token registered in the DB to the given
address by calling `<wrapper_program>::mint_public` on each. Because each
token's admin is the treasury/deployer key, this never requires the treasury
to hold a balance — it mints fresh supply.

Proving each token takes ~15-30s and the list can be long, so this returns
immediately with `status: "running"` + a `job_id`. The mints run in a
detached worker (capped concurrency, per-token timeout); poll
`GET /airdrop/{job_id}` until `status == "complete"` to read per-token
results.

Required env: `DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY` (the treasury/admin of every token;
`AIRDROP_PRIVATE_KEY` honored as a fallback), `RPC_URLS` (first entry used).

422.description: Request body does not match schema

get.description:
Returns the current state of a background airdrop job: `status`
(`"running"` | `"complete"`), the expected `total`, and the per-token
`results` collected so far.

200.description: API tokens for the authenticated wallet (no secrets)
API token created; the token value is shown only once

400.description: Invalid name, expiry, or active-token limit reached

422.description: Request body does not match schema

404.description: Token not found or already revoked

200.description: Pause state for a token pair (order-independent)
Allowlist / pause state for a single token

parameters.name: ticks
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of tick values to verify

200.description: Raw on-chain pool slot + optional tick statuses

get.description:
Batched rolling-24h stats for many pools in one request. Used by the
pools-table page to populate per-row volume / fees / APR without doing
`N + 1` round-trips. Missing keys are silently dropped from the response
(they just won't appear in the returned map); the caller treats absence
as "no stats yet" rather than an error.

Cap: hard limit of 100 keys per request to avoid run-away DB load.

200.description: Map of pool key → 24h stats; failed or unknown keys may be omitted

get.summary:
Returns the full sorted list of *initialized* ticks for the given pool — every
`tick_lower` and `tick_upper` across all non-burned positions, deduplicated.
get.description:
Used by the frontend mint flow to compute the correct
`tick_lower_hint` / `tick_upper_hint` for the AMM's hint-walk asserts.

422.description: Request body does not match schema
Request body does not match schema
Request body does not match schema

parameters.name: amount_in
parameters.description: Optional positive canonical decimal amount for output estimation

404.description: No route or executable quote for the given tokens

503.description: Routing dependencies are temporarily unavailable or the quote candidate set exceeds the safe limit

get.description:
Returns the full list of on-chain trading, liquidity, and token operations
with their input signatures. Clients can use this to build transaction
payloads without hardcoding the Leo function signatures.

200.description: List of all on-chain operation schemas

get.description: Returns the input schema for a single operation (e.g. `swap`, `mint`, `collect`).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Operation ID (swap, mint, create_pool, burn, collect, decrease_liquidity, swap_multi_hop, claim_swap_output, claim_multi_hop_output, register_token, mint_public, mint_private, transfer_public_to_private, add_fee_tier, add_tick_spacing, set_pool_enabled)

parameters.name: pool
parameters.description: Filter by any pool in the route

422.description: Request body does not match schema

post.description:
Deploys a fresh self-contained ARC-20 token (the treasury/deployer key is its
baked-in admin) and mints the initial supply to the creator (`recipient`),
then registers it (with `wrapper_program`) so it is swap- and airdrop-ready.

Deploy + mint are two on-chain txs that take minutes, so this returns
immediately with `status: "deploying"` and finishes in the background. Poll
`GET /tokens` for a row whose `wrapper_program` equals the returned
`program_id` to know it completed.

Required env: `DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY` (treasury/admin; `AIRDROP_PRIVATE_KEY`
honored as fallback), `RPC_URLS`. `leo` must be on the server PATH.

422.description: Request body does not match schema

post.description:
Admin-mints `amount` (base units) of an existing wrapper to `recipient`,
signed by the treasury/deployer key (the token's admin). Synchronous — a
single mint tx. Reuses scripts/airdrop-helper.mjs (which calls mint_public).

422.description: Request body does not match schema

get.description:
Returns everything the user can still claim:
  - Swaps with `claimed_at IS NULL` (user can drive `claim_swap_output`).
  - Positions with `tokens_owed0 > 0` or `tokens_owed1 > 0`
    (post-`decrease_liquidity` accumulations; user calls `collect`).

The indexer is the source of truth: it stamps `claimed_at` for every claim
variant, and the `swaps` row already carries every field the claim tx needs.
So we do zero on-chain mapping reads here — that earlier per-swap RPC loop
was both slow (N sequential reads) and wrong (reads went through a balancer
hitting nodes at different heights, so freshly-claimed swaps flapped back).

AirdropJob.description:
Status of a background airdrop job. `status` is `"running"` until every token
has resolved, then `"complete"`. `results` grows as each per-token mint lands,
so a poller sees live progress (`results.len()` of `total`).

address.description: Aleo address (`aleo1...`) to receive 1000 of each registered token.

AirdropStartResult.description:
Returned by `POST /airdrop` — the airdrop now runs in the background, so the
caller gets a `job_id` to poll `GET /airdrop/{job_id}` with.

token.description: Full secret, returned only once at creation.

wrapper_program.description: Deployed ARC-20 wrapper program ID (e.g. `mtk_a1b2c3.aleo`). Optional.

recipient.description: Aleo address (`aleo1...`) of the creator — receives the initial supply.

supply.description: Initial supply in whole units (default 1_000_000), minted to `recipient`.

status.description:
Always `"deploying"`. Deploy + initial mint run in the background
(minutes); poll `GET /tokens` until a token with this `wrapper_program`
appears to know it finished and was registered.

fee_tier.description: Raw hundredths of a basis point; the fee fraction is this value divided by 1,000,000.

FunctionSchema.description: Full schema for a single trading / liquidity operation on the Aleo AMM.

follow_up.description: Optional follow-up transactions that must be executed after this one.

inputs.description: Ordered list of inputs — index in this array == positional argument index.

program.description: Aleo program ID the function lives on (e.g. `shield_swap_v0_0_1.aleo`, `token_registry.aleo`).

amount.description: Amount in BASE units (already scaled by 10^decimals), as a string.

c.description: Last normalized token1-per-token0 price; execution price after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

h.description: Highest normalized token1-per-token0 price; execution price after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

l.description: Lowest normalized token1-per-token0 price; execution price after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

o.description: First normalized token1-per-token0 price; execution price after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

v.description: Raw token0 volume; exact consumed units after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

high_24h.description: Highest normalized price; execution price after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

low_24h.description: Lowest normalized price; execution price after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

open_24h.description: Earliest normalized price; execution price after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

volume_24h.description: Raw token0 volume; exact consumed units after migration 0028, preserved legacy baseline before it.

SchemaField.description: Describes a single input field of an on-chain function or struct.

fields.description: For struct-typed inputs, the nested field list.

name.description: Parameter/field name as used in the Leo/Aleo program.

type.description:
Aleo type: `field`, `u128`, `u64`, `u32`, `u16`, `u8`, `i128`, `i32`, `bool`, `address`,
a struct name (e.g. `SwapRequest`), or `record` for Aleo records.

visibility.description: `public` or `private` — controls whether the value is revealed on-chain.

hops.description: Full ordered route for single-hop and multi-hop swaps.

pool.description: First-hop pool retained for compatibility; use `hops` for the full route.

trade.description: Pool analytics trade; absent when a route lacks authoritative per-hop amounts.

bearer_auth.description: Session JWT from `POST /auth/verify` (24h), or a long-lived API token (`ss_…`) minted at `POST /api-tokens`. API tokens work on data and trading endpoints; token management and admin endpoints accept session JWTs only.

tags.name: schema
tags.description: On-chain function input schemas for trading / liquidity / token operations

tags.name: api-tokens
tags.description: Long-lived API tokens for programmatic access; send as `Authorization: Bearer ss_…` on any gated endpoint

tags.name: compliance
tags.description: Token/pair allowlist, pause, and pool-creation gating state

tags.name: airdrop
tags.description: Faucet — sends 1000 of every registered token to a user address

tags.name: unclaimed
tags.description: Pending swap outputs and position fees
